Ships: 2200: USS Severn got underway & stood to Ed
1705: USS Nantucket got underway, stood Eastwards

Note: 1100: NWxW1/2W, Sighted Cape Samana brg NWxW 32mi
1645: Anchored. Off Coal Id, Samana Bay.
0700: Fort of Puerto Plata bore S 2mi cc NW

People: 0600: Francis J Worth (ord Sergt HC) departed this life, Post mortem examination held by Asst Sug D Dickinson found cause of death to be Cancer of the large intestine. At 1500 sent funeral party on shore with the body of Serg F. J. Worth for internment.

Other: 2200: Observed several shooting stars to the SxE.

Note: 1700: Got Underway. 1730 Grange Pt bore NE 3/4mi Course NW
1900: Cape Haitian bore SWxW1/4W. 1930 cc WNW1/2W
1600: Cape Maysi bore S 15mi, cc NWxW1/2W
0610: Lucretia Pt bore SWxW1/4W. 0630 cc WNW1/2W
0300: Lobos Lt bore E. cc NWxW1/2W
2310: Bahia de Cadiz Lighthouse bore WSW 20mmi
1500: Key Lh brg NW1/2N, cc NW
1420: Fire reported in fore hold under the Magazine. Determined to be spontanious combustion. Extinguished by own force.
0600: Got Underway. Standing towards the monitor
0745: Moored. Along side of & made fast to the monitor ! Manhatten!
0910: Got Underway. Took monitor Manhatten & towed out of harbor.
1200: Sand Key Lt bore West. Fort Taylor bore NNW1/4W. Noon: Key West.FL
Ships: 1200: Dictator & Ajax, Kansas and Myandolts (??) right ahead - Pilgrim and Sangus (??) on port qtr.

Note: 1500: Got Underway. Made fast to Sangus and started ahead. Course East.
2000: Cape Henlopen Lt brg WxN, Cape May Lt brg NWxN. 2030 standing towards Henlopen Lt
0045: Let go of Monitor & steamed in for Breakwater.
0130: Anchored. Light on end of breakwater brg ExS
1105: Got Underway. Stood out to sea. 1155 took departure Cape Henlopen Lt brg WxS 3mi
1200: SExE. 1230 cc SSE1/4E Noon: Delaware breakwater
1815: Got Underway. 1845 anchored of Naval Hospital.

People: 0145: John Molloy (Pri Marine) fell overboard and was drowned. 0800 Sent parties to drag for the body of John Molloy (Pri Marine) drowned but could not find him. 1600 Sent parties to drag for the body of John Molloy
1600: Found the body of John Molloy floating in the river.
0840: Sent Lieut S.M. Ackley in charge of funeral party to bury remains of John Molloy (PM)
